originalTitle: South Africa president faces call to resign after court ruling
title: Super News from South Africa: Everyone Follows the Rules!
content:
Hello, Super Kids of Africa!

Hi there, future leaders and amazing thinkers! It's your friendly news reporter, here to share some important news from our wonderful continent, Africa! Today, we're zooming into a very special country called South Africa. Do you know South Africa? It's famous for its beautiful wildlife, like lions and elephants, and its amazing people! Did you know? South Africa has 11 official languages! That's a lot of ways to say "hello" and "thank you" to each other!

Who is the President?

Every country has a leader, right? Like a captain of a big team or the head of a family. In South Africa, the leader is called the President. The President helps to make sure everyone is safe, happy, and that the country runs smoothly. Our President in South Africa is a kind man named Mr. Cyril Ramaphosa. He has a very big and important job! Did you know? Presidents in many countries are chosen by the people! When grown-ups vote, they pick who they want to lead their country.

What is Parliament and Who are MPs?

Now, imagine the President needs many helpers to do his big job. These helpers are called Members of Parliament, or MPs for short. They work in a special big building called Parliament. Think of Parliament as a big meeting place where these MPs talk about rules and ideas for the country. They are like a big team that helps the President make good decisions for everyone. Did you know? The Parliament building in South Africa is in a city called Cape Town. It's a very grand place!

What is a Court?

Sometimes, people might disagree about rules, or wonder if rules were followed properly. That's where a "Court" comes in! A court is a special place where special grown-ups, called judges, listen to everyone and decide what is fair and right, based on the rules. It's like a referee in a football game, making sure everyone plays by the rules. Did you know? Judges wear special robes when they are in court. It makes them look very important!

The Constitutional Court – The Super Referee!

In South Africa, there's a very, very important court called the Constitutional Court. This court is like the *super referee* for the most important rules of the country. These super important rules are all written down in a special book called the "Constitution." The Constitution is like the country's main rule book that everyone, even the President and the MPs, must follow. The Constitutional Court makes sure everyone respects this big rule book. Did you know? The Constitution helps make sure everyone in the country is treated fairly and has important rights, like the right to go to school!

A Special Check-Up for the President

Now, sometimes, people want to do a special check-up on the President. This check-up is called "impeachment proceedings." It's a very serious way to check if the President has followed all the rules, especially the big rules in the Constitution. If they find that the President did not follow the rules, he might have to stop being President. It's like a very, very serious report card for the country's leader. Did you know? Leaders of countries have to follow rules just like kids do at school and home! Everyone must follow the rules!

What Happened Recently?

So, here's the news part! Remember the MPs, the President's helpers? Well, a little while ago, they had a chance to start this special check-up, this "impeachment proceeding," for President Ramaphosa. But some of the MPs decided *not* to do it. They stopped the check-up from happening. Then, the very important Constitutional Court, our super referee, looked at what happened. And the Court said, "Uh oh! The MPs were wrong to stop the check-up!" The Court said the MPs *should* have done the check-up. They made a mistake by not following the right steps to check if everything was okay with the President. Did you know? Even grown-ups can make mistakes, and it's important to learn from them!

What Happens Next?

Now, because the Constitutional Court, the super referee, said the MPs made a mistake, the MPs have to go back and do it again. They will need to look very carefully at the rules and decide if President Ramaphosa needs a full special check-up. It means they must make sure they follow all the rules for how to do this check-up. This is important because it shows that no one is above the rules, not even the President or the MPs. Everyone in South Africa, and indeed in any country, must follow the rules in the big rule book, the Constitution. It helps to keep everything fair and just for everyone. So, the MPs will meet again to talk about this. They will make sure to do what the super referee, the Constitutional Court, has asked them to do. It’s like when your teacher tells you to try a task again to make sure you get it just right! Did you know? Many countries in Africa, like Kenya and Nigeria, also have a special rule book called a Constitution!

Why is This Important for You?

This news might sound big and grown-up, but it's important for you too! It shows that in a country, there are important ways to make sure that leaders follow the rules. It means that the rules are important for everyone. It helps make sure that the country is fair and works well for all the children and grown-ups living in it. We will keep watching to see what happens next in South Africa. For now, remember that following rules makes everything fair and good for everyone! Keep shining bright, young leaders!
summary: A big court in South Africa told helpers of the President that they made a mistake by stopping a special check-up on the President, meaning they must do the check-up now.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that everyone, even the country's leaders, must follow the rules to keep things fair and right for everyone in the country.

originalTitle: WHO chief says Hantavirus outbreak ‘is not COVID’
title: Super News! Let's Talk About Hantavirus – It's NOT COVID!
content:
Hello everyone! Your friendly African kid reporter is here with some super important news! Today, we are going to talk about something called **Hantavirus**. Now, I know big words can sound a bit scary, but don't worry! Our super smart doctors and health helpers want us to know something very important: Hantavirus is **NOT** like COVID-19. This is really good news, and I'm going to tell you all about it in easy words!

What is Hantavirus?

Hantavirus is a tiny germ that can make people feel a bit sick. Imagine a super-duper tiny invisible creature. Sometimes, when these tiny germs get into a person's body, they can make them feel unwell. People might get a fever, which means their body feels too hot. They might also feel very tired, or have a tummy ache, or a headache. These are just some ways your body tells you it's fighting off germs. Did you know? Germs are so small, you can't even see them with your eyes! You need a special microscope to see these tiny little things. That's why washing our hands is so important – it helps wash away the invisible germs!

Where Does Hantavirus Come From? (Hint: It's Not People!)

Now, this is the really important part that makes Hantavirus different from other sicknesses we know. Hantavirus germs mostly live in **mice and rats**! Yes, those tiny little animals we sometimes see scurrying around. Not every mouse or rat has these germs, but some do. These germs are in their poop (we call it droppings), their pee, and their spit. When these dry up, tiny bits can float in the air, like dust. If we breathe in that dust, or if we touch something with the mouse germs and then touch our face, we could get sick. That's how it usually happens. It's not like getting sick from your friend who coughs on you. Did you know? Mice and rats love to live where they can find food easily. That's why it's a good idea to always keep our food put away in containers!

How Do We Stay Safe from Hantavirus?

Staying safe from Hantavirus is all about keeping things clean, especially where mice and rats might be! Here are some super tips for you and your family: 1. **Keep Your Home Clean:** Make sure there's no food left out for mice to nibble on. Put food away in cupboards or containers. Clean up any crumbs or spills right away. 2. **Ask Grown-Ups for Help:** If you ever see a mouse or rat, or their tiny droppings, tell a grown-up right away! Grown-ups know how to clean up safely without stirring up the dust that might have germs. 3. **Wash Your Hands!** This is always a great tip for any germ! Wash your hands really well with soap and water, especially after playing outside, or if you've been in a place where mice might have been, and definitely before you eat anything. Sing a song while you wash to make sure you wash for long enough! Did you know? Washing your hands for 20 seconds, about the time it takes to sing 'Happy Birthday' twice, helps wash away most germs!

Is Hantavirus Like COVID-19? NO! Absolutely Not!

This is the biggest and best news! The super smart doctors who help everyone in the world with sickness, like the people at the World Health Organization (we call them WHO for short), have said it clearly: **Hantavirus is NOT COVID-19.** Remember how COVID-19 could spread so easily from person to person? If someone coughed or sneezed, the germs could fly out and go to another person. Well, Hantavirus doesn't do that! It is very, very, very rare for Hantavirus to spread from one person to another person. You mostly get it directly from those tiny mouse or rat germs, not from your friends or family. This means we don't need to worry about Hantavirus spreading around our schools and communities in Africa the same way COVID-19 did. It's a different kind of sickness with different rules. Did you know? The WHO is like a team of doctors and scientists from all over the world, including many smart people here in Africa, who work together to make sure everyone stays healthy! They share important health news with everyone.

What Did the Smart Doctors (WHO Chief) Say?

The person who leads the WHO, a very important doctor, said, **“Hantavirus outbreak ‘is not COVID’.”** This means they looked at the situation, they studied the Hantavirus, and they want to make sure everyone understands that it is a different kind of challenge. They are telling us not to be scared and compare it to COVID-19 because it's just not the same. They want us to know the facts so we can stay calm and safe. These doctors know what they are talking about, and they are here to help us. They are making sure that doctors and nurses in our towns and villages in Africa know how to spot Hantavirus and how to help people who might get it. It's a rare sickness, so it's not something we hear about every day, but it's good to know how to stay safe. Did you know? Doctors and nurses are like real-life superheroes! They work so hard to help us feel better when we are sick and teach us how to stay healthy.

What Should YOU Do, My Young Friends?

So, what does all this mean for you, my amazing young listeners? It means: * **Don't be scared!** Hantavirus is not something that will spread like wildfire from person to person. * **Keep your spaces clean.** Help your grown-ups keep your home tidy, especially from crumbs. * **Wash those hands!** Always, always, always wash your hands with soap and water. * **Tell an adult.** If you ever see mice or their droppings, or if you feel very sick, always tell a parent, guardian, or teacher. They will know what to do. Remember, we have smart doctors and health workers all over Africa and the world who are watching out for us. They are working hard to keep everyone safe and healthy. So, let's all do our part by staying clean and listening to our grown-ups! This is your kid reporter, signing off for now, stay safe and healthy everyone!
summary: Smart doctors from around the world say that Hantavirus, a sickness you can get from mice, is very different from COVID-19 and does not spread easily from person to person, so we don't need to worry about it like COVID.
whyMatters: This news is important because it helps us understand that even though there are different sicknesses, Hantavirus is not as scary or widespread as COVID-19, and by keeping clean and washing our hands, we can stay safe.
